user_1781904690153.message: "The fatigue feels like I haven't slept in days - heavy, dragging. I've had it for about 3 weeks now. I'm also experiencing headaches, difficulty concentrating, and I'm more irritable than usual. I have no fever or other physical symptoms."

I don't have a thermometer on hand right now, but I don't feel feverish (no chills, sweating, or flushed skin). Based on how I feel, I'd estimate it's normal—probably around 98.4–98.6°F (36.9–37.0°C). I haven't checked it recently, though.
oughly 3 to 4 weeks now. It started gradually—first the fatigue, then the headache and brain fog followed about a week later. The irritability has been more noticeable in the last 7–10 day

've also noticed that my symptoms improve significantly on weekends when I'm not working—less screen time, more movement, and less stress. That makes me think a large component is lifestyle/stress-related, though I still want to rule out organic causes with blood work.

• It's possible that your symptoms are related to a combination of factors, including lifestyle, stress, and potentially underlying health conditions. • Identifying and addressing these factors can help you better manage your symptoms and overall well-being.

My diet is vegetarian with likely low iron/B12—I eat decently but don't supplement specifically. I'm mostly sedentary now, sitting 8+ hours daily with almost no exercise, though I used to walk regularly. Sleep is disrupted—I wake up at 2–3 AM and 5 AM most nights, feeling unrefreshed even with 7+ hours in bed. Recent stressors include a tight work deadline and a family member's hospitalization, both piling up over the past month. Family history includes hypothyroidism (mother), type 2 diabetes (father), and anemia (mother), so I'm mindful of those possibilities.
